text.title: Agent Lookup Protocol — Index First, Grep Last
text.summary: The project AGENTS.md declares the index path and forces agents to consult it before any text grep, with grep allowed only for non-symbol literals.

section.title: Required lookup order
section.p: Coding agents default to grep because it is universal. The cost is enormous on big repos — every grep hit returns surrounding lines, all loaded into context. The symbol index returns one structured span per symbol, which is what the agent actually wants.

rule.statement: The project `AGENTS.md` MUST declare the symbol index path and required lookup order: agents query the index for any "where is `<Symbol>` defined", "find references to `<Symbol>`", or "what calls `<Symbol>`" question; agents fall back to grep ONLY for non-symbol literals (strings, regex patterns, comments). Coding-agent system prompts (Claude Code skill, Cursor rule, Codex `AGENTS.md`) MUST embed this protocol verbatim. PR templates MUST include a checkbox: "Symbol-index queries used where applicable".
rule.rationale: Forcing the index to be the first lookup cuts agent context cost on real refactors by 5-20x (Cognition Devin and Cursor team published numbers in 2025-2026). The grep fallback for non-symbol literals stops the rule from being absolute and unworkable. Embedding the protocol in `AGENTS.md` and the PR template gates both runtime behaviour and review behaviour with the same source of truth.

example.code:
# AGENTS.md ## Code Lookup Symbol index lives at `tags` (universal-ctags, drift-checked).
Agents MUST: 1. For symbol queries (definition, references, callers) — query `tags` via `readtags` or Serena MCP. 2. For non-symbol literals (strings, comments, regex) — `Grep` with a tight glob. 3. NEVER bulk-grep the repo for a symbol when the index is available.
example.note: The agent has a deterministic decision: index for symbols, grep for literals.

example.code: # AGENTS.md "Use whatever search tool feels natural."
example.note: The agent picks grep every time; context blows up; refactors miss references that the index would catch instantly.

antipattern.description: Letting each agent pick its own search tool ad-hoc per task.
antipattern.reason: Behaviour becomes non-reproducible across agent kinds (Claude Code vs Cursor vs Codex), and context cost explodes on the largest repos exactly when it matters most.

section.reference: Pre-commit hook that keeps the index fresh.
`readtags(1)` — fast O(log n) lookup CLI agents can shell out to.
Serena MCP server — exposes symbol queries directly to MCP-aware agents.
Cognition Devin context post — symbol-index miss as the dominant context-bloat source.
